mission.location: Kuala Lumpur HQ + offshore rotations (Layang field, offshore Sarawak)

reasons: 12 years of offshore process engineering in Malaysian waters (PETRONAS Carigali, Hess Malaysia) — directly transferable to ENEOS Xplora's Layang field operations.
Led three brownfield debottlenecking campaigns delivering +8% to +15% throughput gains — the exact remit of this role.
HAZOP chair on five revamp projects, fluent with PETRONAS PTS and DEP standards already in force on Layang.
Already collaborated with the Bintulu shore-base operations team in 2022, shortening onboarding by an estimated 2–3 weeks.
Based in KL, no relocation required, available from 1 June with offshore rotations up to 21 days.

match.need: Min. 10 years offshore Malaysia process engineering

match.answer: 3 campaigns led: dehydration +12%, gas compression +8%, separator train +15%

match.need: HAZOP leadership and PETRONAS PTS familiarity
match.answer: HAZOP chair on 5 revamp projects, PTS daily-driver since 2015
Daily use for 10+ years; trained 4 junior engineers on both
Confirmed — available 1 June, KL resident, family settled, no relocation needed

qualification.motivation: After a 12-month consulting break, the candidate wants to return to upstream operations on a technically meaningful asset. He sees Layang as the most rewarding brownfield play in Sarawak right now, with a clear runway for production optimization work over the next 24 months.
verbatim: "Brownfield is where engineering actually moves the needle — greenfield is where you draw, brownfield is where you deliver."
"On Layang specifically, the dehydration train is the obvious lever — I'd want to look at the data in the first month before committing to a number."
"I'm not looking for a title bump, I'm looking for an asset I can actually improve."
soft_skills: Structured every project description in STAR format without being prompted.
Spontaneously cited two past safety incidents and what he changed afterwards.
Asked sharp questions about ENEOS's decision authority on capex below MYR 5M.
Comfortable with offshore rotations but flagged a hard cap of 21 days due to family in KL.
qualification.notes: Family settled in KL, will not accept rotations longer than 21 days. Available 1 June at earliest (current consulting engagement runs to end-May). Salary expectation aligned with MYR 1,800 / day; would consider 1,750 for a 12-month committed term.

profile_summary: Senior process engineer with 12 years of offshore upstream experience in Malaysian waters, specialised in brownfield debottlenecking and production optimization. Track record of unlocking double-digit throughput gains with zero or minimal capex, HAZOP chair on five revamp projects.

experience.title: Senior Process Engineer
experience.context: North Malay Basin gas development — FPSO Bergading plus four satellite platforms supplying gas to Peninsular Malaysia under a long-term PETRONAS GSA.
actions: Led debottlenecking of the TEG dehydration train, unlocking +12% gas export capacity with zero capex.
Chaired HAZOP for the 2022 compression revamp (3 trains, 60 MMSCFD each).
Built the asset's first integrated production model in PIPESIM, used weekly by operations.
Mentored four junior engineers on HYSYS, HAZOP facilitation and PETRONAS PTS application.
On-call process engineer for offshore — handled 14 unplanned shutdowns over the period.
experience.environment: Aspen HYSYS, PIPESIM, Flarenet, PI System — API 521, API 14C, PETRONAS PTS

experience.title: Process Engineer
experience.context: Sabah upstream operations, multiple offshore platforms feeding the Kimanis Gas Terminal.
actions: Day-to-day process support for three offshore platforms.
Operations interface for two greenfield tie-ins (engineering, commissioning, start-up).
Co-authored revisions to internal PTS sections on flare design.
Delivered safety case updates for two assets ahead of regulatory review.
experience.environment: Aspen HYSYS, Flarenet, Visual Flow — PETRONAS PTS, API 521, API RP 14E

experience.title: Graduate Process Engineer
experience.context: EPC support to a PETRONAS LNG Train 9 (Bintulu) module fabrication package.
actions: Heat-exchanger sizing, hydraulic checks and flare load contributions.
Vendor document review and technical query handling.

additional: Co-author, two internal PETRONAS PTS revisions on flare design (2017, 2018).
Speaker, OGA Kuala Lumpur 2023 — "Zero-capex debottlenecking in mature gas assets".
Volunteer mentor, Young Engineers programme, IEM Malaysia.

contact.name: Quentin Cloarec
contact.about: Founder of Trees Engineering, placing senior engineers across APAC energy projects. 10+ years connecting offshore Oil & Gas talent with operators in Malaysia and the Middle East.
